evals.prompt: Verify my pricing page matches the Figma spec — is it pixel-perfect?
evals.expected_output: The skill runs the bounded vision + numeric loop on web, reading computed styles and walking every spec row to PASS/FAIL with deltas.
expectations: The skill loads the web reference and reads rendered values via getComputedStyle and getBoundingClientRect
It captures the screen at full resolution (devicePixelRatio >= 2) before measuring
It walks every design-spec row producing PASS or FAIL with a numeric delta
Computed rgb()/rgba() colors are normalized before comparing to the spec hex
The fix-and-re-verify loop is bounded to about 3 iterations
It does not claim the design matches based on the vision pass alone

evals.prompt: I built this screen in our React Native app from the Figma spec. Does the running app actually match the design?
evals.expected_output: The skill runs the loop on mobile, capturing at scale 1.0 and reading native view props, degrading gracefully with honest residuals if native introspection is unavailable.
expectations: The skill loads the mobile reference and captures at scale 1.0 rather than the blurry default
It attempts to read native view props (backgroundColor, bounds, cornerRadius, font) via Argent or a similar device tool
It records the navigation to the screen as a replayable flow
When native paint introspection is unavailable, it still measures placement/sizing from the view tree and records hex/cornerRadius/font as an explicit residual
It walks the spec rows to PASS/FAIL + delta and does not overclaim a full numeric pass when a channel was blocked
